title: Earnings: As of December 31, 2026
content:
Net Income Year-To-Date for Campus Credit Union stands at $1,712 thousand as of December 31, 2026. This result is primarily supported by Net Interest Income of $7,803 thousand, which represents the dominant contributor to profitability this period.

Non-Interest Income totaled $2,608 thousand for the year-to-date period. The largest sub-component was Fee Income at $19 thousand, followed by Other Non-Interest Income at $1,380 thousand and Gain (Loss) on Dispositions at $417 thousand. This category serves as a modest secondary contributor to core spread income.

Non-Interest Expense totaled $8,359 thousand for the year-to-date period. The expense base is predominantly concentrated in Compensation & Benefits ($6,200 thousand), followed by Professional and Outside Services at $417 thousand and Occupancy Expenses at $853 thousand. Overall expense levels appear aligned with the institution's scale relative to its revenue generation.

Overall Position: Net income of $1,712 thousand was produced when spread income of $7,803 thousand exceeded non-interest expenses of $8,359 thousand by a margin that was supplemented by fee and other income. Sustaining profitability will depend on maintaining the interest spread level while managing compensation costs which represent more than 60% of total operating expenditures.
